title: Meta Financial Summary From 2025 Form 10-K
text: Meta's 2025 Form 10-K showed a large and highly profitable core advertising business, along with major investment spending. Full-year revenue was $201.0 billion, up from $164.5 billion in 2024. Income from operations was $83.3 billion, and operating margin was 41%. Net income was $60.5 billion. Family of Apps generated $198.8 billion of revenue, including $196.2 billion of advertising revenue, while Reality Labs generated $2.2 billion of revenue. Meta ended 2025 with $81.6 billion in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities and $58.7 billion of long-term debt. The company spent $72.2 billion on capital expenditures, including finance lease principal payments, reflecting heavy investment in servers, network infrastructure, data centers, AI, and Reality Labs hardware. Key risks include regulatory scrutiny, privacy and content moderation issues, competition for users and advertisers, dependence on ad revenue, high AI infrastructure costs, Reality Labs losses, cybersecurity, and uncertainty around new AI and immersive products.
